contact.address: 1 Lorong Mambong #01-14 Holland Village Market & Food Centre Singapore 277700

reviews: Minced Meat Noodle | $4 They have increased in price from $3.50 to $4 but still as good hence no complaints.
Ba Chor Mee [~$3.50] Digging in to my favourite bowl of BCM with lots of vinegar and springy QQ mee...
Minced Meat Noodle | $3.50 One of my go to for minced meat noodle. Noodle was springy with generous...
Generous bowl of Bak Chor Mee at $4.00. . Location: Ming Fa FishBall (Holland Village, 1 Lor Mambong...)
Amazing bowl of Bak Chor Mee with generous portion of noodles, minced meat and condiments. The ...
stalls.stall_name: Ming Fa Fishball (Holland Village Market & Food Centre)

reviews.author: Kai Lee
reviews.text:
Easily one of the best bak chor mee spots I’ve tried! The moment I finished my first bowl, I was already craving another. The noodles were perfectly cooked, the sauce was rich and flavourful, and the ingredients were super generous.

The meatball was huge, and the double meat filling was a pleasant surprise, juicy and bursting with flavour the moment you bite into it. Truly a satisfying bowl from start to finish. Highly recommended for anyone who loves bak chor mee! Pricing was decent for the portion as well.

reviews.author: Matthew Lee
reviews.text:
Visited this place on a weekend for lunch. The place was crowded and there was a queue at the stall.
Waited for about 20 minutes before it was my turn. The wait wasn't too long cause there were quite a number of noodle dishes to choose from and took me some time to finally decide what I wanted.
First opted for the bak chor mee then decided to upsize to the Jumbo one since I came all the way here. And since I took 45 minutes here, might as well get the biggest portion available.
If you ask me, the prices here are really reasonable. It's quite a steal to even have chicken cutlet in a bowl of noodles for $5.50 when a typical bowl of plain bak chor mee can cost $5 easily.
I was quite shocked when I got the noodles. The bowl was almost filled to the brim and the first look you can't see the noodles at the top and was just a nice presentation of the ingredients.
Took the chopsticks and mixed it around to realize the huge portion of noodles they have given. The soup might look plain but it was actually quite flavourful.
Pretty decent bowl of noodles if you ask me, would say almost everything was good.
From the photos I saw there wasn't egg and I looked at the addons menu there wasn't an option to add egg so was surprised to see the egg though was not sure was it because I ordered the large one.
Then the disappointment came when the egg turned out to be the flaw of the dish. I wasn't sure if the eggs were freshly cooked by the yolks were definitely overcooked and hard and even had a little bitter taste to it. The texture was almost like the salted egg yolk which I didn't like and worse it affected the noodles as I mixed the yolk to it.
Overall still decent, would have been better if not for the egg yolk. I hope it's just an one off encounter. Worth a try.

reviews.author: Elaine C
reviews.text: Great place to get a taste of the classic Bak Chor Mee plain or embellished with katsu chicken. My favorite noodle option is the Mee Pok, flat egg noodles. Brings back memories of school canteen food

reviews.author: Nic
reviews.text:
very big portion for $5.50! love the variety of ingredients - handmade meatballs, fuzhou fishball, liver, minced pork, sliced pork, mushroom
added fish dumplings for $1.20 too

noodles were not alkaline or mushy and soup was tasty too

reviews.author: Sander van Vliet
reviews.text: Fine chicken cutlet noodles. Not super special but worth it. The amount of food is sufficient for a good evening meal. The shops in this hawker center close pretty early though.

contact.address: 1 Lorong Mambong #01-11 Holland Village Market & Food Centre Singapore 277700

reviews: Simple Breakfast. Bee hoon + noodles + vegetables + luncheon meat + five spice roll (ngoh hiang) = S$3.60.

econ bee hoon/holland. This corner stall has been seeing long queues and loyal customers over the years. This corner stall always has people lining up to buy their fried bee hoon/mee and choosing a plethora of ingredients to go with it.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $3.00. Fried bee hoon with 3 fried eggs, cabbage and cuttlefish fillet.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $3.80. Stir fry vegetables, 3x fried eggs and 2x sotong fillet on top of white bee hoon.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $4.30. Stir fry vegetables, 2x fried eggs, 1x luncheon meat and 1x siting fillet on top of white bee hoon.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $2. Had fish fillet, stir fry cabbage, egg. Slightly moist bee hoon which I liked.
stalls.stall_name: Holland V. Fried Beehoon (Holland Village Market & Food Centre)

reviews.author: Leeanne O'Donnell
reviews.text:
dont let the very pale looking bunch of bee hoon that doesnt look very appetising decieve you, this is actually a very decent and tasty light tasting bee hoon! very nice to have in the morning that wont overwhelm you.

ordered bee hoon + veg, fish fillet & zai er for just $3.60, very decently priced for today's day and age. i would rather they give abit more chilli than give chilli + green chilli, since they do charge extra 20c for more chilli, but i guess valid since the chilli is actually nice and has a good kick to it. veg portion is big, fish fillet is nice and not over-fried, zai er kept crunchy despite only eating it 30 mins after buying. bee hoon portion may be small to some, i initially thought so too but after finishing my meal i realised im full.

did not try the noodle this time, will try again in the future. also want to try some other toppings that looked good.

sry no picture, was too hungry hahaha

reviews.author: Ida Einjaw
reviews.text: The only food stall opened in the morning. Price is ok for food in Holland village. good bee hoon, fried chicken,sunny side up eggs with runny yolks, fish cakes are good too. The kopi directly opposite the stall is good too, only $1.50 for kopi c kosong. No crowd in the morning because no stalls are opened.

reviews.author: Bryce Tan
reviews.text: Was served soggy beehoon when I came by at lunch time. It was unfortunate, given that they were almost finished selling their noodles, and the chicken wings were all sold out by the time I came by. While the price is reasonable, the soggy noodles left a bad enough impression that I won't be back.

reviews.author: Roland Lim
reviews.text: Portion was good, taste was nostalgic. For a Upsized bee hoon, with a fried egg, slice of luncheon meat, and some veggie, I was charged $3.20, by today's hawker centre prices It is reasonable.

reviews.author: Taste & See
reviews.text:
2024: not as cheap now. $4.50 for 4 ingredients, non meat. Taste is same.

$6.60 for chicken wing, long fish cake, meatballs and mock duck.

2020:Cheap and good. Messy looking though. Tastes different from all other typical economy bee hoon

contact.address: 1 Lorong Mambong #01-15 Holland Village Market & Food Centre Singapore 277700

reviews: Nasi Lemak | $5.00. The rice was so fragrant with coconut. It was soft and fluffy and I was able to taste the coconut milk...
Premium Laksa [~$4.50]. The laksa gravy was slightly diluted but the prawns was on point. Fresh and crunchy...
Nasi Lemak | $5.80. Chicken thigh set with additional fish fillet. Set comes with chicken thigh, vegetables, fried egg, peanuts and ikan bilis.
Fantastic and Affordable Nasi Lemak. Famous Nasi Lemak from Holland Village. For an affordable price of $4-$6, it offers high value.
Good Enough Nasi Lemak. This nasi lemak stall has been here since the beginning of times and the standards have remained pretty consistent.
363 Holland Village Nasi Lemak. Weekend breakfasts are made of these. Oozy egg atop fluffy, fragrant coconut rice.
stalls.stall_name: 363 Katong Laksa Holland Village Nasi Lemak (Holland Village Market & Food Centre)

reviews.author: see you latté
reviews.text:
Upon entering Holland Village Food Centre, the fragrant aroma of laksa leads me to 363 Katong Laksa👍👏😋

It's past peak lunch hour @1.30pm when I arrive at the stall so no long line, unlike the previous time I was here. Menubis straightforward- just Laksa (Standard or Premium) and Nasi Lemak (Set Meals) with add-on and special buy options. Ordering each specialty is done separately, i.e., queue at the side for Laksa and in front for Nasi Lemak.

Lady boss is friendly and patient when taking my order. My bowl of steaming hot Premium Laksa is filled almost to the brim with fresh ingredients - sliced fish cake, blood cockles, boiled prawns, halved tofu puffs (tau pok), crunchy beansprouts (tau geh) and delicate thick rice vermicelli* (bee Hoon), topped with chopped laksa leaves.

*Bee Hoon here not cut up; need to use chopsticks.

Wanting to taste the laksa broth as it is, I request to omit the spicy sambal. The gravy is coconut-rich, flavorful with decent unami-ness and balanced consistency and mouthfeel👍👏😋 Manageable heat level, ideal for those with lower tolerance for spiciness. Spice-loving ones can add a dollop or even extra sambal for a more robust punch of spices.

A pretty good bowl of Premium Laksa that's value for money👍👏😋

reviews.author: Yogeswaran Gopala Krishnan
reviews.text:
Hey, it’s a hawker centre, so atmosphere is going to depend on the availability of tables 😅.

There are mainly 2 versions of Nasi Lemak in Singapore. The Malay version which is the original as basically Nasi Lemak is a Malay food.

Then, there is the Chinese take on Nasi Lemak. This stall serves the Chinese version. The aunty is friendly and obliges to requests like additional sambal.

I took Nasi Lemak ala carte, with fried chicken, long beans and the usual sides of fried egg, anchovies and peanuts.

The rice was fragrant, chicken (boneless) was crispy and the rest of the sides also  complements well. The price…SGD6, which is very reasonable for the portion.

As I write, the line is forming which only tells me that this stall is a hit. Apart from Nasi Lemak, they also have Katong Laksa, which I am going to try next time.

Overall, great Nasi Lemak, a real nice comfort food to start my late Sunday morning.

p.s.

I was hungry and forgot to take the picture of my order. I will try to remember to participate in the Asian culture of taking a photo of what you eat before taking the first bite 😅. Next time.

reviews.author: Elaine C
reviews.text: Great place to satisfy a craving for laksa. The otak is also good. Make sure to queue along the side wall for this to avoid queuing twice.

reviews.author: Y
reviews.text:
I was hesitant to try this place because of the bad reviews but I saw quite a few people eating their nasi lemak on several occasions and it looked good so I decided to give it a try.

I ordered the chicken thigh set ($6) which comes with extra vegetables on top of the standard items. The auntie was very generous with the chicken and ikan billis + peanuts. Rice was decently fragrant, wished there was abit more chili but overall a solid and affordable plate of nasi lemak! Would return if I’m in the area!

reviews.author: Doraemon
reviews.text:
Kitty and I were planning a leisure cycling trip around the west side of Singapore, but the rain put a damper on our plans.  We decided to take the MRT to Holland Village instead, and we were looking for a place to have a late lunch.  We stumbled upon 363 Katong Laksa, which I had heard was closed down.  It was exciting to see it back in action.  It brought back a wave of nostalgia. I remember enjoying their laksa years ago.

I ordered a big bowl of laksa, which cost $6.80 and could be paid digitally.  The laksa was flavorful and comforting, and I enjoyed the generous portion.  It wasn't the most mind-blowing laksa I've ever had. However still tasted OK.  However, the price point in a market setting felt a bit on the expensive side.
